decision_path.text: Troubleshoot Relay/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed

text: Start at the Top Illuminator Circuit Breaker Switch [CB14]; if it is in the OFF position, switch the circuit breaker to ON [CB14], then check whether [CB14] remains in the ON position; if it does not stay ON, troubleshoot the relay or short-circuited wiring and repair as needed, then repeat the functionality test.

Begin by inspecting the Top Illuminator Circuit Breaker Switch [CB14]; if it is in the OFF position, switch Circuit Breaker [CB14] to the ON position, then verify whether [CB14] remains in the ON state, and if it does, proceed to repeat the functionality test.

decision_path.text: Troubleshoot Power Supply [PWS100]/open wiring. Repair as needed

text: If the Top Illuminator Circuit Breaker Switch [CB14] is in the ON position, proceed to troubleshoot the Power Supply [PWS100] for open wiring and repair as needed, then repeat the functionality test.

decision_path.text: Check Ventilation and Fans Let power supply cool down.

text: Starting at the 'Start' step, the first action is to check the Power Supply LED [G1,G2,G3]. If the LED is on, the process concludes here. decision_path.text: Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed

decision_path.text: Troubleshoot 110 VAC supply circuit (Check outlet to the SICK cabinet). Repair as needed

text: Start by checking the Power Supply LED [G1,G2,G3]; if it is off, proceed to the Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [F11,F12,F13] and ensure it is turned on, then verify if the switch remains in the ON position, if it does, troubleshoot the 110 VAC supply circuit (check the outlet to the SICK cabinet) and repair as needed, then repeat the functionality test.

decision_path.text: Switch Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [F11,F12,F13] to ON
Troubleshoot Power Supply/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed

text: Start by checking the Power Supply LED [G1,G2,G3]; if it is off, inspect the Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [F11,F12,F13] and switch it to ON if it is off, then verify if the switch remains ON; if it does not stay ON, troubleshoot the Power Supply or short-circuited wiring and repair as needed, then repeat the Functionality Test.

text: Start by checking the Power Supply LED [G1,G2,G3]; if it is ON, proceed to examine the Circuit Breaker L[F111,F113,F115], R[F121,F123,F125], B[F131,F133,F135], and if these are also ON, troubleshoot open wiring and repair as needed.

decision_path.text: Troubleshoot open wiring to Camera/Illumilator. Repair as needed

text: Begin by checking the Power Supply LED [G1,G2,G3]; if it is ON, proceed to inspect the Circuit Breaker L[F111,F113,F115], R[F121,F123,F125], B[F131,F133,F135]. If these are OFF, switch them to ON and verify if they remain ON; if they do, address any open wiring issues to the Camera/Illumilator and repair as needed.

decision_path.text: Power Cycle and verify power. Replace if continues

text: Starting at the 'Start' step, the process checks whether the device ready light is on; if the device ready light is not on or shows red, the next action is to power cycle the device and verify the power connection, replacing the device if the issue persists.
Start by checking if the device ready light is on; if it is illuminated green, proceed to verify if the service light is illuminated, and if the service light is on or flashing, replace the camera.

decision_path.text: Power cycle and watch illuminator during power up.
Verify Power connection and repeat test. Replace if still unresponsive.

text: Start by checking if the device ready light is on; if it is, proceed to verify if the service light is illuminated, and if not, power cycle the device while observing the illuminator during startup, then assess the illuminator's functionality, and if it remains inactive, confirm the power connection and repeat the test, replacing the device if it remains unresponsive.

decision_path.text: Power cycle and watch illuminator during power up.
Does light come on when system is triggered?

text: Start by checking if the device ready light is on; if it is, proceed to verify if the service light is illuminated. If the service light is not illuminated, power cycle the device and observe the illuminator during startup. Next, evaluate what the illuminator does and determine if the light activates when the system is triggered. If the light does not come on during activation, verify and correct the CAN wiring before retesting.

decision_path.text: Power cycle and watch illuminator during power up.
Verify illumination connection and repeat. Replace camera if light still stays on.

text: Start by checking if the Device Ready Light is on; if it is, proceed to verify if the service light is illuminated. If the Device Ready Light is not on, power cycle the device and observe the illuminator during startup. Once the illuminator's behavior is assessed, if it remains lit, verify the illumination connection and repeat the process; replace the camera if the light continues to stay on despite these steps.

decision_path.text: Power cycle and watch illuminator during power up.
Does light come on when system is triggered?
Does Result light flash when valid barcode facing camera? No
Power cycle and repeat test. Have programming verified if continues

text: Start by checking if the device ready light is on; if it is, proceed to verify if the service light is illuminated. If the light comes on during activation, check if the result light flashes when a valid barcode faces the camera; if it does not, power cycle the device and repeat the test, having programming verified if the issue persists.

decision_path.text: Power cycle and watch illuminator during power up.

text: Start by checking if the device ready light is on; if it is, proceed to verify if the service light is illuminated. If the device ready light is not on, power cycle the device and observe the illuminator during startup. Begin by examining the X8 – CAN "Act" LED; if it flickers during operation, proceed to recheck the operation.

Start by checking the X8 – CAN "Act" LED if it is off proceed to examine the 4 CAN wires then verify if the 4 wires are attached if not attach the wires and test again if the system is working correctly resume operation.

decision_path.text: Check All CAN connection on all cameras and VMS. Connect cable or replace cable as needed

text: Start by examining the X8 – CAN "Act" LED; if it is off, proceed to examine the 4 CAN wires and verify if they are attached, then check all CAN connections on all cameras and VMS, connecting or replacing cables as needed, and confirm if the system is working correctly, resuming operation if it is.

decision_path.text: Replace the terminator for X8 - CAN

text: Start at the step 'X8 – CAN “Act” LED'; if the LED is off, examine the 4 CAN wires and check if they are attached if not, attach the wires and test again; if the system is not working correctly, replace the terminator for X8 - CAN.

decision_path.text: Check All CAN connection on all cameras and VMS. Connect cable or replace cable as needed
Replace the terminator for X8 - CAN

text: Start by examining the X8 – CAN "Act" LED; if it is off, proceed to examine the 4 CAN wires and verify if they are attached, then check all CAN connections on all cameras and VMS, connecting or replacing cables as needed, and if the system is not working correctly, replace the terminator for X8 - CAN.
Starting at the "Start" step, the next action is to check the "Power" LED X2. If the LED is off, the process directs to the "Power Supply TS" step.

decision_path.text: CAN – Device is missing. Check CAN Connection/Device

text: Start by checking the "Power" LED X2; if it is ON, proceed to the "Device Ready" LED, and if that LED is Green, move to the "System Ready" LED; however, if the "Power" LED X2 is OFF, the CAN – Device is missing, so check the CAN Connection/Device.

decision_path.text: Device is busy. Application (SE & CE) will not run properly. Reboot system.

text: Starting at the "Start" step, check the "Power" LED X2; if it is on, proceed to the "Device Ready" LED, and if this LED is green, advance to the "System Ready" LED, then to the "Function 1" LED if it is also green; however, if the "Power" LED X2 is off, the device is busy, preventing the application (SE & CE) from running properly, requiring a system reboot.

Start by checking the "Power" LED X2; if it is on, proceed to the "Device Ready" LED, and if this LED is green, move to the "System Ready" LED, then to the "Function 1" LED; if the "Function 1" LED is white, proceed to the "Trigger Input" LED, X4 Input A, otherwise, if the "Power" LED X2 is off, go to "Trigger Input TS".

decision_path.text: Yes Do both “Encoder Input” LEDs remain constantly on when the conveyor belt is running? X5

text: Start by checking the "Power" LED X2 if it is on proceed to the "Device Ready" LED if this LED is green proceed to the "System Ready" LED if this LED is also green proceed to the "Function 1" LED if the "Function 1" LED is white proceed to the "Trigger Input" LED X4 Input A if the condition "ON/OFF for every Package" is met proceed to the question "Do both 'Encoder Input' LEDs remain constantly on when the conveyor belt is running" X5 if the answer is no proceed to "Encoder Input TS".

decision_path.text: Cycle power to SIM Controller. If issue persists, replace Controller

text: Start by checking the 'Power' LED X2; if it is on, proceed to the 'Device Ready' LED, and if it is off, cycle power to the SIM Controller, and if the issue persists, replace the Controller, then repeat the System Functionality Test.
Begin at the "CAN" LED X8-CAN; if the LED is off, proceed to the "CAN TS" step.

decision_path.text: Check the physical Ethernet connection on both ends of the cables’s connection points

text: Start at the "CAN" LED X8-CAN, and if the LED flickers during operation, proceed to examine the "ETHERNET 1-4" LEDs X9–X12; if any of the conditions indicate an issue, check the physical Ethernet connection on both ends of the cable's connection points.

decision_path.text: Cameras light up when package enters tunnel

text: Start at the "CAN" LED X8-CAN; if the LED flickers during operation, proceed to check the "ETHERNET 1-4" LEDs X9–X12; if the LED remains steadily illuminated, move to verify that cameras light up when a package enters the tunnel; if the condition is not met, navigate to the "Camera TS" step.

decision_path.text: Cameras light up when package enters tunnel

text: Start at the "CAN" LED X8-CAN; if the LED flickers during operation proceed to check the "ETHERNET 1-4" LEDs X9 – X12 if the LEDs remain consistently green proceed to verify that the cameras light up when the package enters the tunnel if the system functionality test confirms proper operation repeat the system functionality test.

decision_path.text: Check Ventilation and Fans Let power supply cool down.

decision_path.text: Switch Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10] to ON

decision_path.text: Troubleshoot 110 VAC supply circuit (Check outlet to the SICK cabinet). Repair as needed

text: Start by examining the Power Supply LED if the system fails to initialize; if the LED is OFF, inspect and reset the Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [CB1], whereas if the LED is ON, proceed to troubleshoot the 110 VAC supply circuit by checking the outlet connection to the SICK cabinet and repairing any issues, then repeat the functionality test to confirm resolution.

decision_path.text: Switch Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [CB1] to ON
Troubleshoot Power Supply/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed

Verify whether [CB1] remains ON; if it does not, troubleshoot the Power Supply or short-circuited wiring and repair as needed. Once resolved, repeat the Functionality Test.

decision_path.text: Switch Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [CB1] to ON
Troubleshoot 110 VAC supply circuit (Check outlet to the SICK cabinet). Repair as needed

decision_path.text: Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed

decision_path.text: Switch Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10] to ON
Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed

text: Start by examining the Power Supply LED if it is ON proceed to check the Power Supply LED Color if it is GREEN proceed to Circuit Breaker/ Camera Switch [CB10] if the Power Supply LED is OFF switch the Switch Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10] to ON then verify if [CB10] stays ON if it does proceed to check if Fuse [FU122] is intact if it is not proceed to troubleshoot short-circuited wiring and repair as needed.

Starting at the Start step, check the Power Supply LED if the Power Supply LED is ON proceed to check the Power Supply LED Color if the Power Supply LED Color is GREEN proceed to the Circuit Breaker/ Camera Switch [CB10] if the Circuit Breaker/ Camera Switch [CB10] is ON proceed to Troubleshoot Relay [CR100] repair as needed.

decision_path.text: Switch Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10] to ON
Troubleshoot open wiring to SIM2000. Repair as needed

text: Begin by checking the Power Supply LED; if it is ON, proceed to verify its color, and if the color is GREEN, move to Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10]; if the LED is OFF, switch Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10] to ON, then confirm whether [CB10] remains ON, and if it does, check if Fuse [FU122] is intact, and if so, troubleshoot open wiring to SIM2000 and repair as needed.

decision_path.text: Check Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080)

text: Start by checking the Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080); if the speed is off by less than 30%, proceed to clean the encoder wheels.

decision_path.text: Check Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080)

text: Start by checking the Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080) if the displayed speed is correct proceed to recheck operation.

If cable and Encoder both replaced and the speed is still not correct, replace the SIM2000

decision_path.text: Check Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080)
If cable and Encoder both replaced and the speed is still not correct, replace the SIM2000

decision_path.text: Check Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080)

decision_path.text: With the path to the reflector clear is the yellow light on?
Does the yellow light go off when a package is present?
Verify and correct alignment of sensor to reflector
Teach sensitivity. Press Teach-in button >2s. Both LED indicators illuminate constantly.
Test again. If it still not working correctly, replace the sensor

text: Start by checking if the green light is on; if yes, proceed to verify if the yellow light is on with the path to the reflector clear, then determine if the yellow light turns off when a package is present, if not, align the sensor to the reflector, next teach the sensor sensitivity by pressing the teach-in button for more than two seconds until both LED indicators illuminate constantly, and finally test again, replacing the sensor if the issue persists.

decision_path.text: With the path to the reflector clear is the yellow light on?
Check the reflector and the sensor
Verify and correct alignment of sensor to reflector

Test again. If it still not working correctly, replace the sensor

text: Start by checking if the green light is on; if it is, proceed to verify whether the yellow light is on with the path to the reflector clear, and if the yellow light is not on or blinking, inspect the reflector and sensor, then ensure the sensor and reflector are properly aligned, followed by teaching the sensitivity by pressing the teach-in button for more than two seconds until both LED indicators remain constantly illuminated, and finally test the system again, replacing the sensor if it still does not function correctly.

decision_path.text: With the path to the reflector clear is the yellow light on?

If it does, verify the SIM programming and functionality, replacing it if necessary.

decision_path.text: With the path to the reflector clear is the yellow light on?
